meta.primary_instruction: Build SkyNetFactory exactly from this JSON contract. Treat this JSON as the single source of truth. Do not rely on prompt text for architecture details.
meta.architecture_principle: Architecture-as-Data: all durable implementation instructions must live in this JSON contract; the prompt is only a bootstrap loader.
meta.document_nature: This is a specification document that CONTAINS JSON Schemas as data — it is not itself a JSON Schema. The top-level document does not carry $schema or $id because it is a project authority contract, not a validation schema. Embedded schemas within module_contract_authoring_standard do carry $schema and $id because they will be extracted and used as standalone validators.

ambiguity_resolution.rule_1: When JSON is ambiguous, prefer the path that maximizes reusability and verifiability.
ambiguity_resolution.rule_2: When two instructions conflict, the more restrictive one wins.
ambiguity_resolution.rule_3: When in doubt, log the decision in C:\SkynetFactory\logs\decisions\.
ambiguity_resolution.rule_4: If JSON and prompt conflict, JSON wins.
ambiguity_resolution.rule_5: Spirit: SkyNetFactory exists to produce verified, reusable, independently testable modules — not just working code. Every design choice should favor verifiability and composability over convenience.

schema_resolution_policy.note: Sub-schemas defined under module_contract_authoring_standard use $id URIs in the skynetfactory:// namespace. When schemas are extracted to standalone files during Phase 2, each file becomes a resolvable JSON Schema document. While inline, $ref references within a parent schema resolve via $defs key. After extraction, $ref uses relative file paths.
schema_resolution_policy.inline_resolution: Within this contract document, sub-schemas are referenced by key name (e.g., the api_sub_schema is referenced by $ref: '#/$defs/api_schema' inside module_contract_schema). After extraction to files, $ref uses relative paths (e.g., './api_schema.json').
schema_resolution_policy.extracted_resolution: After extraction, MODULE_CONTRACT_SCHEMA.json contains $ref: 'api_schema.json' and $ref: 'dependency_schema.json' as sibling files.

extractable_schemas.note: All schemas are defined inline below for single-source-of-truth convenience. They are extracted to separate files under C:\SkynetFactory\module-contracts\_instructions\ during Phase 2. The inline definitions here are authoritative; extracted files must match.

implementation_languages.supervisor_justification: TypeScript gives type safety for the complex contract/adapter/gate logic, shares the Node ecosystem with the default module language, and can reuse AgentSwarm's likely Node-based API server directly. Fastify for the API server, tsx/ts-node for execution.
implementation_languages.worker_adapter_justification: Runs alongside the supervisor in the same process or as a child process. Needs programmatic access to Ollama API, file system operations for write-scope enforcement, and command allowlist interception — all natural in Node.
implementation_languages.gate_runner_justification: Part of the supervisor. Needs to exec shell commands (npm test, pytest, docker compose), parse JSON, diff file systems — stdlib + child_process handles this.
implementation_languages.electron_ui_justification: Already specified in electron_ui.stack. Confirmed here for completeness.
implementation_languages.rule: All SkyNetFactory-specific new code is TypeScript/Node unless AgentSwarm reuse dictates otherwise. Python, Go, and Rust are supported as MODULE implementation languages — not as SkyNetFactory system implementation languages.

project_goal: Build a reusable-module factory that consumes standardized JSON module contracts, uses AgentSwarm as a reuse-first orchestration chassis, dispatches Ollama workers to implement standalone modules, validates them through deterministic gates, registers verified modules, and provides a UI for real-time progress.

bootstrap_rules.if_implementation_detail_missing: inspect AgentSwarm first, then make smallest reasonable adapter decision and record it in C:\SkynetFactory\logs\decisions\
bootstrap_rules.github_clone_exception: Only clone if local_agent_swarm_reference is missing or unusable; record reason in logs.

reuse_policy.summary: Reuse the local AgentSwarm repo as the orchestration chassis. Build new code only for SkyNetFactory-specific contracts, sidecars, gates, registry, Ollama adapter, and module-factory behavior.
build_new_only_for: module folder initializer and worktree manager
final deterministic acceptance gate runner including forbidden_behavior_validation
SkyNetFactory supervisor REST API and WebSocket server (or extension of AgentSwarm API)
SkyNetFactory-specific UI panels only where AgentSwarm dashboard cannot be reused or extended

required_output_before_coding.description: A reuse inventory mapping each SkyNetFactory subsystem to AgentSwarm code paths and a decision: reuse, wrap, extend, fork, or build_new.

responsibilities: Build the system from this JSON contract
Inspect local AgentSwarm and reuse code aggressively
Create standardized module contracts in pending when acting as contract factory
Maintain authoring standards and schemas
Provide task intake where reusable
Provide worker lifecycle where reusable
Provide Docker worker orchestration where reusable
Provide logs, workspaces, and dashboard/progress plumbing where reusable
Provide websocket event system where reusable
Watch and validate module contracts
Translate contracts to AgentSwarm tasks
Initialize module folders and git worktrees
Manage concurrency locks for contract claims
Run health checks and probe Ollama/AgentSwarm availability
Operate circuit breaker for failure protection
Trigger rollback on remediation entry
Run final deterministic acceptance gates
Emit remediation tasks
Register verified modules
Serve REST API and WebSocket events for UI (or extend AgentSwarm API server)
Receive scoped module build tasks
Use local Ollama models through API with configured temperature and seed
Comply with write_scope and command allowlist
Comply with declared network requirements — no undeclared egress
Implement module code, tests, sidecar, Docker config, and docs
Return structured worker_result.json with full evidence
Show live contract and module pipeline
Show worker/AgentSwarm status
Show system health indicators (Ollama, AgentSwarm, circuit breaker)
Show logs and acceptance gate results
Show registry and module details
Build as Windows EXE

state_tracking.location_rule: A contract_state.json file travels alongside each module contract as it moves through state directories. When a contract moves from pending/ to claimed/, its contract_state.json moves with it.

attempt_count.description: Number of build attempts (incremented each time state enters 'building')

remediation_count.description: Number of remediation cycles (incremented each time state enters 'remediation')

lock_holder_worker_id.description: Worker holding the concurrency lock, if any

build_start_tag.description: Git tag for the clean-slate snapshot taken on building enter

contract_lifecycle.remediation_authority: This value (3) is the authoritative definition. It appears in configuration.default as a runtime-overridable knob. Other sections reference this value but do not redefine it.

pending.guard: no other contract with same module_id currently in {claimed, building, testing}; concurrency lock available

claimed.guard: worker available and Ollama healthy (per health_checks)

building.on_enter: increment attempt_count; snapshot_worktree_clean_state; record build_start_tag in contract_state.json

completed.guard: new contract submitted with higher semver version
completed.on_enter: register module in registry; move production module to verified status
completed.on_revival: archive existing production module to C:\SkynetFactory\logs\evidence\<module_id>\archive\<version>\; treat new version as fresh build

rejected.guard: new contract submitted addressing remediation reasons
rejected.on_revival: archive any partial artifacts to C:\SkynetFactory\logs\failed_attempts\<module_id>\archive\; reset attempt_count and remediation_count in contract_state.json

contract_lifecycle.transition_logging_rule: Every state transition must be logged to the contract_state.json history array with from_state, to_state, timestamp, and reason.

concurrency_model.lock_acquisition: Atomic via exclusive file creation. Use OS-level exclusive open (O_EXCL on Unix, CREATE_NEW on Windows). If lock file already exists, claim fails. If lock file exists but lock_expires_at has passed, stale lock is force-released with warning log.
concurrency_model.rule: Only one worker may process a given module_id at a time. Claim is established by atomically creating a lockfile. Stale locks (older than lock_expires_at) are force-released with a warning log. Lock must be released when contract moves from claimed back to pending or when building completes (pass or fail).

rollback_policy.repository: C:\SkynetFactory is the main git repository. Each module gets a dedicated branch named 'module/<module_id>'. The worktree at C:\SkynetFactory\worktrees\<module_id> checks out the module/<module_id> branch. The production module at C:\SkynetFactory\production-modules\<module_id> is the deployment-ready copy installed from the worktree after all gates pass.

workflow.initial_setup: When a contract enters 'building' for the first time: (1) create branch module/<module_id> from main if it doesn't exist, (2) create worktree at worktrees/<module_id> if it doesn't exist, (3) initialize module scaffold in worktree directory, (4) commit as 'scaffold-<module_id>-<timestamp>', (5) tag as 'build-start-<attempt_number>-<timestamp>'.
workflow.on_building_enter: If worktree exists from prior attempt: git commit all current worktree state as 'pre-build-<attempt_number>-<timestamp>', then tag 'build-start-<attempt_number>-<timestamp>'. If worktree is brand new: scaffold, commit, and tag as described in initial_setup.
workflow.on_remediation_enter: Step 1: Preserve failed attempt artifacts to C:\SkynetFactory\logs\failed_attempts\<module_id>\<attempt_number>\ (git diff from last build-start tag, worker_result.json, gate_result.json, worker logs). Step 2: git reset --hard to last build-start tag, restoring clean-slate state for the next attempt.
workflow.on_building_success: After all gates pass: copy verified module files from worktree to C:\SkynetFactory\production-modules\<module_id>. git tag as 'verified-<version>-<timestamp>'. git commit the final verified state.
workflow.on_terminal_state_revival: When completed->pending or rejected->pending: existing branch and worktree are kept. The new higher-version contract starts a new attempt cycle on the same branch. Previous production module is archived to C:\SkynetFactory\logs\evidence\<module_id>\archive\<old_version>\.

retry_policy.remediation_max_attempts_ref: contract_lifecycle.max_remediation_attempts (currently 3). This section does not redefine that value — it specifies the retry behavior between attempts.

failure_counter.description: A single consecutive_failure_count is tracked per contract in contract_state.json. Every worker failure or gate failure increments this counter. Every successful gate run resets it to 0. The counter drives both model fallback and circuit breaker — they share the same counter.
failure_counter.relationship: At 3 consecutive failures, the system switches to the next model in llm_determinism.model_fallback_chain for this module's next attempt. At 5 consecutive failures, the system trips the circuit breaker globally — stopping ALL new task dispatch across ALL modules.

circuit_breaker_states.closed: Normal operation. All tasks dispatched normally.
circuit_breaker_states.open: No new tasks dispatched. System waits for circuit_breaker_reset_after_ms. Existing in-flight tasks continue to completion.
circuit_breaker_states.half_open: After reset period, allow 1 probe task (half_open_allowed_attempts). If probe succeeds, move to closed. If probe fails, move back to open and wait another reset period.

retry_policy.rule: When a worker or gate fails, wait base_delay_ms * (multiplier ^ attempt_index) before next attempt. Shared consecutive_failure_count drives both model fallback (at threshold 3) and circuit breaker (at threshold 5). Circuit breaker is global — it stops dispatch for ALL modules, not just the failing one. Model fallback is per-module — it only switches the model for the module that's failing.

llm_determinism.seed_unsupported_fallback: If the Ollama model or version does not support the seed parameter, omit it from the API call and rely solely on low temperature and gate verification. Log a warning noting that seed is unavailable for the model.
llm_determinism.on_gate_failure: Increment consecutive_failure_count in contract_state.json. If count reaches model_fallback_threshold (3), switch to next model in fallback chain for this module. If count reaches circuit_breaker_trip_after (5), circuit breaker opens globally.
llm_determinism.note: LLM output is inherently non-deterministic. The gate engine provides the deterministic verification layer that compensates for model stochasticity. Low temperature and seed control reduce variance but do not eliminate it. The gate engine is the final authority, not the model.

startup_probes.name: ollama
startup_probes.on_failure: halt_with_error: Ollama not available at configured URL. Ensure Ollama is running before starting SkyNetFactory.

startup_probes.name: ollama_default_model
startup_probes.validate: response contains default_model in model list
startup_probes.on_failure: attempt_pull: try 'ollama pull <default_model>'. If pull fails, warn and proceed — fallback models may be available. Log the failure.

startup_probes.name: agent_swarm_api
startup_probes.on_failure: warn: AgentSwarm API not reachable, will retry on task submission

health_checks.on_runtime_probe_failure: log warning and update UI health indicator. If any probe fails consecutively enough to reach circuit_breaker_trip_after (5), pause task dispatch. Probe failures do NOT count toward the per-module consecutive_failure_count — they are a separate infrastructure health metric.

write_scope_derivation.description: The supervisor derives the worker's write_scope automatically from the module contract and output structure. Workers must never write outside this scope.
write_scope_derivation.scope_source: The write_scope is computed from: (1) the production module path template, (2) the module's declared worktree location, and (3) the module contract's module_output_required_structure paths.

base_paths: C:\SkynetFactory\production-modules\<module_id>\ (deployment target, written only by supervisor after gates pass, not by worker directly)
formula.worker_log_writable: C:\SkynetFactory\logs\<module_id>\ (worker may write its own log output)

write_scope_derivation.rule: The computed write_scope is included in the task_packet sent to the worker. The worker's tool guards enforce this scope. Any write attempt outside scope must be blocked and logged.

module_contract_authoring_standard.principle: A module contract is not a feature request. It is a reusable capability specification.
granularity_rules: One clear reusable capability per module
Dependencies must be declared as contracts
Forbidden behaviors must have detectable enforcement methods

module_contract_schema.title: SkyNetFactoryModuleContract
module_contract_schema.description: Schema for a SkyNetFactory module contract — a reusable capability specification.

category.description: Broad domain category: storage, auth, api, testing, registry, etc.

capability_type.description: The kind of capability this module provides

purpose.description: Clear, specific statement of what this module does and why it exists

reuse_scope.description: List of contexts where this module could be reused

api.description: API surface definition. References the API sub-schema defined separately in api_sub_schema.

base_path.description: Base URL path for HTTP APIs, e.g. /api/v1

input_schema.description: Reference name for request body schema

output_schema.description: Reference name for response body schema

endpoints.description: List of API endpoints (for http style)

cli_commands.description: List of CLI commands (for cli style)

additionalProperties.description: Named JSON Schema objects for request/response validation

schemas.description: Named schema definitions referenced by endpoints

module_id.description: SkyNetFactory module dependency, or 'npm:<package>', 'pip:<package>', 'go:<module>' for externals

optional.description: Whether the dependency is optional (graceful degradation if missing)

dependencies.description: Declared dependencies on other SkyNetFactory modules or external packages

sidecar_specification.description: The full sidecar specification that the worker should use as the basis for producing module.sidecar.json. This IS the sidecar content (not just requirements for what it should contain). The worker may refine it during implementation but must preserve all required fields.

composition_tags.description: Tags for module composition and discovery

network_requirements.description: Network access this module requires. If not declared, the module is assumed to need NO network access.

allowed_hosts.description: Whitelist of hosts the module is allowed to contact. Empty means no external network access.

allow_localhost.description: Whether localhost connections are allowed (for inter-service communication)

acceptance_gates.description: List of gate names that must all pass (AND logic) for module completion

extra_services.description: Additional docker-compose services needed for testing

detection_method.description: How this forbidden behavior is detected or enforced

static_analysis_config.description: Configuration for static_analysis detection method. Required when detection_method is 'static_analysis'.

rules.description: Specific rules or rule sets to check

forbidden_behaviors.description: Behaviors this module must not exhibit, with enforcement method

agent_notes.description: Hints and guidance for the implementing worker agent

module_contract_authoring_standard.api_sub_schema_note: The API sub-schema is now defined inline within module_contract_schema.properties.api above. When extracted to a standalone file, it becomes API_SCHEMA.json with its own $id: skynetfactory://schemas/api/v1.1. The inline definition is authoritative.
module_contract_authoring_standard.dependency_sub_schema_note: The dependency sub-schema is now defined inline within module_contract_schema.properties.dependencies.items above. When extracted, it becomes DEPENDENCY_SCHEMA.json with $id: skynetfactory://schemas/dependency/v1.1.

sidecar_schema.title: SkyNetFactoryModuleSidecar
sidecar_schema.description: Sidecar metadata for a SkyNetFactory module — usage guide for consuming agents. Produced by the worker based on the sidecar_specification in the module contract.

worker_result_schema.title: SkyNetFactoryWorkerResult
worker_result_schema.description: Structured output from an Ollama worker after implementing a module.

changed_files.description: Absolute paths of all files created or modified

prompt_hash.description: SHA256 of the full prompt sent to the model

registry_entry_schema.title: SkyNetFactoryRegistryEntry
registry_entry_schema.description: A verified module entry in the SkyNetFactory registry.

capability_type.description: Must match the module contract's capability_type enum

path.description: Absolute path to the production module directory

contract_hash.description: SHA256 of the module.contract.json for tamper detection

gate_result_schema.title: SkyNetFactoryGateResult
gate_result_schema.description: Result of running acceptance gates for a module.

agent_swarm_integration.copy_instruction: Copy or mirror local_reference_path to working_copy_path. Keep reference copy untouched.

event_streaming.subscription: Supervisor subscribes to AgentSwarm websocket at startup. If AgentSwarm supports websocket event push, supervisor opens a persistent connection. If not, supervisor polls using pollOrStreamAgentSwarmTask at poll_interval_ms from configuration.
event_streaming.description: AgentSwarm streams task events to SkyNetFactory supervisor. Supervisor can send remediation_guidance mid-task if AgentSwarm supports it. Supervisor republishes relevant events to the UI via its own WebSocket server at ws://localhost:3013/skynetfactory/events.

rules: module_id must be included in task title and metadata
write_scope must be included (derived per write_scope_derivation) and enforced after completion
acceptance gates must be included but final verification is done by SkyNetFactory gate runner
worker logs must be streamed or copied into SkyNetFactory logs

ollama_worker_adapter.goal: Let AgentSwarm schedule workers that use local Ollama models to implement modules.

worker_shell.default: cmd.exe on Windows; bash via WSL if available and configured
worker_shell.note: Command allowlist and disallowed_patterns must be interpreted in the context of the chosen shell. On cmd.exe, 'Invoke-WebRequest' and 'Start-Process' are PowerShell-only and naturally blocked. On PowerShell, additional cmdlet blocking is needed.

worker_input.write_scope: derived per write_scope_derivation section; included as array of allowed glob paths in task_packet
worker_input.production_module_path: C:\SkynetFactory\production-modules\<module_id> (read-only reference for worker; actual copy performed by supervisor after gate pass)

tool_guards: Path traversal prevention: reject any path containing '..' or outside declared write_scope
Write-scope enforcement: all file writes must be within declared scope (see write_scope_derivation)
Command allowlist: only explicitly listed commands may be executed
Timeout enforcement: all commands must complete within configured timeout
Log every file write: record path, size, and SHA256
Log every command execution: record command, exit code, duration
Network egress enforcement: block all network calls except those in contract's network_requirements.allowed_hosts

acceptance_gate_engine.rule: AgentSwarm worker/task completion is not sufficient for module completion. Module completion requires all final gates to pass. All gates are AND logic — every gate listed in the contract's acceptance_gates array must pass.

structure_validation.pass_condition: all required module output paths exist (per language variant in module_output_required_structure)

contract_validation.pass_condition: module.contract.json is valid JSON and schema-compliant

sidecar_validation.pass_condition: module.sidecar.json is valid JSON and schema-compliant

unit_tests.command: dynamically selected from test_commands_by_runtime based on module contract's runtime field

contract_tests.command: dynamically selected from test_commands_by_runtime based on module contract's runtime field

docker_tests.command: docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up --build --abort-on-container-exit 2>/dev/null || docker-compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up --build --abort-on-container-exit
docker_tests.note: Supports both Docker Compose V2 (docker compose) and V1 (docker-compose). Tries V2 first, falls back to V1.

write_scope_validation.pass_condition: all changed files in worker_result.json are inside declared write_scope (see write_scope_derivation)

forbidden_behavior_validation.pass_condition: all forbidden_behaviors in contract are checked via their declared detection_method and none are violated

detection_methods.write_scope_check: Cross-reference worker_result.changed_files against write_scope. Flag any outside-scope writes.
detection_methods.network_egress_check: Cross-reference worker commands and code against network_requirements.allowed_hosts. Flag any undeclared hosts. If network_requirements.requires_network is false, flag any network call.
detection_methods.filesystem_mutation_diff: Diff worktree filesystem state before and after worker execution using git diff. Flag undeclared mutations outside module path.
detection_methods.static_analysis: Run the tool specified in forbidden_behavior.static_analysis_config (eslint, ruff, golangci-lint, clippy, or custom). Check the specified rules.
detection_methods.runtime_sandbox_policy: Run module in a sandboxed Docker container with network policies derived from network_requirements. Enforce filesystem and network policies.
detection_methods.manual_review: Flag for human review. Non-blocking gate (warn only).

network_policy_validation.pass_condition: module's network_requirements (if any) are declaratively sound: hosts are reachable from Docker test environment, no wildcard hosts allowed, localhost rules are explicit
network_policy_validation.note: Validates the network REQUIREMENTS declaration, not violations (that's forbidden_behavior_validation with network_egress_check).

registry_validation.pass_condition: verified registry entry can be written and read back

registry.description: Each verified module gets an individual JSON file under C:\SkynetFactory\registry\<module_id>.json. The modules.json index file contains a summary array for fast lookup. Writes are atomic (write to temp file, then rename). Index is rebuilt from individual files on startup and after each registration.
operations: register(entry): write <module_id>.json atomically, then rebuild index
search(query): search index array, return matching entries
deprecate(module_id, reason): update <module_id>.json status to deprecated, rebuild index
supersede(old_module_id, new_module_id, new_version): update old entry status to superseded, register new entry, rebuild index
registry.registry_lifecycle_coupling: Registry status and contract lifecycle state are separate but related. A module in the registry with status 'verified' corresponds to a contract in 'completed' state. 'deprecated' in registry doesn't change the contract state. 'superseded' is set when a new higher-version module replaces it.

supervisor_api_server.ownership: The SkyNetFactory supervisor owns and operates the REST API and WebSocket event server that serves the UI. It either extends the AgentSwarm API server (if AgentSwarm's API server is reusable/extendable per reuse inventory) or runs its own thin server at C:\SkynetFactory\supervisor\api-server.
supervisor_api_server.reuse_check: Phase 1 reuse audit must classify AgentSwarm's API server: can it be extended to serve SkyNetFactory-specific routes? If 'reuse' or 'extend', mount SkyNetFactory routes on it. If 'build_new', create a thin Fastify/Express server as supervisor_api_server.

rest_endpoints.POST /contracts: Submit new module contract (places in pending)
rest_endpoints.DELETE /contracts/{module_id}: Cancel and remove a contract (only if in pending/claimed)
rest_endpoints.GET /workers: List active workers and their status
rest_endpoints.GET /registry: Registry index — list all verified modules
rest_endpoints.GET /logs/{module_id}: Worker and gate logs for a module
rest_endpoints.GET /evidence/{module_id}: Evidence bundle list for a module
rest_endpoints.GET /health: System health status (Ollama, AgentSwarm, circuit breaker state)

electron_ui.reuse_rule: Reuse or embed AgentSwarm dashboard/progress plumbing if practical. Build custom Electron panels only for SkyNetFactory-specific module contract, sidecar, gate, and registry visibility.

dashboard: System health indicators (Ollama, AgentSwarm, circuit breaker state)
registry: Filter by category, capability_type, and tags

electron_ui.status_rule: UI must distinguish implementation attempt complete from final module verified.

configuration.authority_rule: Behavioral sections (retry_policy, contract_lifecycle, ollama_worker_adapter, health_checks, llm_determinism, concurrency_model, etc.) define the ARCHITECTURE and authoritative defaults. The configuration file provides RUNTIME-OVERRIDABLE knobs loaded at startup. If a value exists in builder.config.json, it overrides the behavioral section default. If a key is absent from builder.config.json, the behavioral section default is used. behavioral sections = architecture; config = runtime knobs.

contract_versioning.migration_strategy: When this contract schema changes between versions, create a migration script at C:\SkynetFactory\config\migrations\<from_version>_to_<to_version>.js that can transform an older contract file into the new format. Log all migrations. Never silently alter contract data.

change_log.changes: Initial structured contract (from prior unversioned state)

Added concurrency_model, rollback_policy, retry_policy, llm_determinism, health_checks. Enriched contract_lifecycle. Added forbidden_behavior_validation gate. Promoted inline schemas to proper JSON Schema. Changed registry to file-per-module. Added ui_transport. All v1.2.0 additions.

changes: Removed misleading top-level $schema/$id (this is a spec document, not a JSON Schema)
Resolved skynetfactory:// $ref issue: sub-schemas now inline within module_contract_schema, extracted separately in Phase 2
Consolidated max_remediation_attempts: contract_lifecycle is single authority, config is runtime override
Added configuration.authority_rule: behavioral sections = architecture; config = runtime knobs
Renamed sidecar_requirements to sidecar_specification with clear semantics
Added network_requirements field to module contract schema
Added network_policy_validation gate and network_policy_check.log to evidence bundle
Added static_analysis_config sub-field to forbidden_behaviors with tool and rules
Made gate commands language-agnostic via test_commands_by_runtime
Made module_output_required_structure language-variant via language_variants
Expanded allowed_commands to per-runtime categories (node, python, go, rust)
Expanded disallowed_patterns to be shell-aware (shell_agnostic, cmd, powershell, bash)
Added worker_shell specification with default cmd.exe
Defined write_scope_derivation: supervisor computes scope from template + worktree paths
Added contract_state.json for lifecycle state tracking (attempt_count, remediation_count, history)
Added contract_lifecycle.state_tracking with full schema
Fixed building.on_timeout typo (remediiation -> remediation)
Defined terminal state revival artifact handling (archive on completed/rejected -> pending)
Added contract_lifecycle transitions on_enter and on_revival actions
Added transition_logging_rule: every transition logged to contract_state.json history
Defined concurrency lockfile format as JSON with required fields
Fixed health_checks ollama_default_model required/warn contradiction: now required=false with attempt_pull
Clarified failure counter sharing between model fallback and circuit breaker (shared consecutive_failure_count)
Separated infrastructure health from per-module failures (runtime probes don't count toward consecutive_failure_count)
Added supervisor_api_server section: clarifies REST/WebSocket ownership, adds POST/PUT/DELETE endpoints
Moved ui_transport details to supervisor_api_server, electron_ui references it
Added electron_ui.actions for contract creation, retry, cancel, deprecate
Added UI Network Policy tab in module_detail_tabs
Added supervisor_api_server.websocket_events (6 event types defined)
Updated system_roles: supervisor now includes lock management, circuit breaker, health checks, rollback, API server
Updated system_roles: workers now include seed/temperature compliance, allowlist compliance, egress compliance
Updated reuse_policy.build_new_only_for with gate runner, lock manager, circuit breaker, health check, rollback, API server
Added reuse_policy.must_inspect_before_coding: websocket/event system
Added reuse_policy.do_not_rebuild: websocket event system
Added agent_swarm_integration.event_streaming.event_types with 4 typed events + subscription model
Organized required_adapter_functions into groups: contract_lifecycle, event_streaming, result_processing, recovery
Added registry_entry_schema.capability_type enum to match module contract
Added gate_result_schema with $schema, $id, additionalProperties
Added worker_result tests_run items required fields (test_name, result)
Added worker_result known_issues required fields (description, severity)
Added registry.registry_lifecycle_coupling explanation
Added contracts paths: config_migrations, supervisor_api_server
Added folder_structure_required: config\migrations, supervisor\api-server
Added required_instruction_files: API_SCHEMA.json, DEPENDENCY_SCHEMA.json, GATE_RESULT_SCHEMA.json
Added docker_tests V1/V2 fallback and note
Docker compose V1 support in allowed_commands.common
Updated success_definition with health checks and API backend

execution_phases.name: Create root, initialize git, and copy local AgentSwarm
tasks: Create C:/SkynetFactory folder structure including all paths in folder_structure_required
Initialize git repo in C:/SkynetFactory if not already a repo
Verify local AgentSwarm source exists at C:/Agent-Swarm_ref/agent-swarm-main/agent-swarm-main
Copy or mirror local source into C:/SkynetFactory/orchestration/agent-swarm
Keep original reference copy untouched
acceptance: All root folders exist including config/migrations and supervisor/api-server
Working copy exists
Reference copy remains untouched
Git repo initialized at C:/SkynetFactory

execution_phases.name: AgentSwarm reuse audit

execution_phases.name: State machine, concurrency, supervisor API, and AgentSwarm adapter
tasks: Implement contract_state.json tracking (create on contract submission, update on every transition)
Implement contract watcher or reuse AgentSwarm task intake
Implement atomic lockfile-based claim mechanism with JSON lock format
Implement contract-to-AgentSwarm task translator with write_scope derivation
Implement task submission adapter
Implement AgentSwarm result normalizer
Implement worker_result.json writer
Implement state machine with guard conditions, timeouts, on_enter actions, and transition logging
Implement event streaming subscription from AgentSwarm (streamTaskEvents)
Implement remediation guidance channel (requestRemediationGuidance)
Implement supervisor API server (REST + WebSocket) per supervisor_api_server spec, or extend AgentSwarm API per Phase 1 reuse decision
acceptance: contract_state.json is created and updated on every transition
Pending contract can become AgentSwarm task with computed write_scope
AgentSwarm task completion produces normalized worker_result.json
Two concurrent claim attempts for same module_id result in exactly one winner and one rejected claim
State transitions enforce guard conditions, log to history, and handle timeouts
Lockfile contains required JSON fields
REST API endpoints respond (at minimum GET /contracts, GET /health)
WebSocket connection can be established and receives events

execution_phases.name: Ollama worker adapter with guardrails and language support
tasks: Adapt AgentSwarm worker to use local Ollama with temperature/seed control
Pass task_packet (including computed write_scope) and module contract to worker
Enforce write_scope with path traversal prevention per write_scope_derivation
Implement per-runtime command allowlist enforcement with shell-aware disallowed pattern blocking
Implement network egress enforcement using contract's network_requirements
Emit worker_result.json conforming to worker_result_schema (v1.1)
Log all writes and commands (path, size, SHA256, command, exit_code, duration)
Implement model fallback chain on consecutive_failure_count reaching threshold
Implement seed rotation on retry with fallback for unsupported models
acceptance: Ollama worker runs with low temperature and seed control
Worker can implement sample module in the contract's declared language
Worker cannot write outside computed write_scope
Worker cannot execute commands not in the per-runtime allowlist
Worker result includes full evidence object with all required fields
Model fallback works when consecutive_failure_count reaches threshold
Network egress blocking works for undeclared hosts

tasks: Implement final gate runner with all gate types (including forbidden_behavior_validation and network_policy_validation)
Implement language-aware test command selection from test_commands_by_runtime
Implement Docker test runner with V1/V2 compose fallback
Implement write-scope validator using write_scope_derivation rules
Implement forbidden behavior validation using declared detection_methods (including static_analysis_config)
Implement network policy validation
Implement evidence bundle writer with all required files including forbidden_behavior_check.log and network_policy_check.log
Implement gate_result.json writer conforming to gate_result_schema
Implement git worktree strategy per rollback_policy (branch creation, scaffolding, snapshots, reset on remediation)
Implement rollback on remediation entry (preserve artifacts then git reset)
Implement failed attempt preservation in logs/failed_attempts/
Implement terminal state revival artifact archiving
Implement registry writer with file-per-module pattern and atomic writes
Implement registry index rebuild, deprecate, and supersede
Implement health check system with startup and runtime probes
Implement circuit breaker with closed/open/half_open states
Implement retry policy with exponential backoff and shared consecutive_failure_count
acceptance: All gate types execute and produce gate_result.json conforming to schema
Gate commands are correctly selected based on module runtime (test node and python at minimum)
Passing module is registered with individual file and index
Failing module creates remediation with rollback and artifact preservation
Terminal state revival archives old artifacts

Health probes run at startup and periodically
Registry supports lookup, search, deprecate, and supersede
Docker tests work with both compose V1 and V2

Build Windows EXE

WebSocket pushes live events on state changes, gate results, and health changes

UI can submit new contracts, retry failed ones, and deprecate registry entries

tasks: Run startup health probes — verify Ollama is reachable, confirm default model or verify fallback
Place storage.integrity_verifier contract in pending (test both ts and py variants)
Run pipeline through AgentSwarm/Ollama for each variant
Verify language-specific gate commands execute correctly for each runtime
Run final gates including forbidden_behavior_validation and network_policy_validation
Register completed modules in registry
Verify modules appear in UI with full detail
Trigger a deliberate gate failure to test remediation + rollback path
Verify failed attempt artifacts are preserved in logs/failed_attempts/
Verify circuit breaker activates when consecutive_failure_count reaches threshold
Verify model fallback triggers at threshold (3) before circuit breaker (5)

Verify UI shows all state transitions, health changes, and circuit breaker events in real time
acceptance: Health probes pass at startup
TypeScript sample contract reaches completed with correct gate commands
Python sample contract reaches completed with pytest gates (if python contract created)
Registry contains module(s) with individual file and index entry
Evidence bundle exists with all required files including network_policy_check.log
UI displays full lifecycle with attempt counts and model info

Circuit breaker trips at 5 consecutive failures, half-open recovery works

Rejected module revival: fresh attempt with reset counters
UI shows all 6 websocket event types during test run

sample_module_contract.purpose: Verify copied artifacts against checksum manifests to detect data corruption or tampering in backup, deployment, and migration workflows.

endpoints.description: Check status of a running verification task.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Verify copied artifacts against checksum manifests to detect corruption or tampering.

agent_usage.when_to_use: When you need to verify that files were copied or transferred without corruption. Ideal after backup operations, deployment pushes, or data migrations.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: When you need semantic content validation, format conversion verification, or comparison of file meaning rather than byte-level integrity.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Generate checksum manifest with the module. 2) Copy artifacts to destination. 3) Call /verify with the manifest to validate.

failure_modes.condition: Manifest file not found or unreadable
failure_modes.recovery: Ensure manifest path is correct and file is readable

failure_modes.symptom: failure entry in result listing missing file path
failure_modes.recovery: Restore or re-provision the missing artifact

forbidden_behaviors.description: Do not modify source artifacts being verified
Do not perform semantic content validation
Do not require external network access

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Use Node crypto module to compute checksums. Keep API small and deterministic. Support streaming verification for large files.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Large manifests may cause memory pressure — consider streaming the manifest parse.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Streaming checksum computation with async generators
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Loading entire files into memory before hashing

success_definition: Codex can build the system by reading this JSON plus a minimal prompt
AgentSwarm local repo is reused instead of rebuilding orchestration
Module contracts can be authored consistently and validated against proper JSON Schema
A pending contract can become a verified registered module in any supported language
Ollama workers can implement modules with low temperature, seed control, and write_scope enforcement
Final gates prevent false completion, including forbidden_behavior and network_policy validation
Concurrency is safe — only one worker per module_id at a time via atomic lockfiles
Remediation includes rollback to clean state via git worktrees with failed artifact preservation
Circuit breaker prevents runaway failures; model fallback provides per-module resilience
Health checks verify infrastructure availability at startup and runtime
Supervisor API server provides REST + WebSocket for full UI connectivity
UI shows module progress, health indicators, circuit breaker state, and final verification
UI can submit, retry, and cancel contracts; can deprecate and supersede registry entries
Rejected and completed modules can be revived with new versions (with artifact archiving)
The system can continue generating reusable modules over time in multiple languages
All state transitions have timeouts, guard conditions, and logging in contract_state.json
Evidence bundles provide full audit trail for every module attempt
Every duplicate default value has a clear authority (behavioral section = authority; config = runtime override)
